message:
fcp-raptorq: validate decode bounds and distinguish duplicate vs conflicting symbols

RaptorQDecoder::receive_symbol had several admission-control gaps:

  - No check that the declared transfer_length fits within
    config.max_object_size, letting an adversarial source block header
    allocate decoder state for objects larger than the configured cap.
  - No check that self.k (source symbols) is within the upper bound
    implied by max_object_size, letting a miscrafted OTI coerce the
    decoder into holding symbol counts it would never accept from the
    encoder.
  - Duplicate ESI handling was "first writer wins, quietly": if two
    different payloads arrived for the same ESI the second was
    dropped without comment. That collapses two distinct failure
    modes (benign retransmission vs. adversarial conflict) into one,
    hiding the latter from observability.
  - No direct-buffer capacity check before inserting into
    `received`, so pathological senders could grow the BTreeMap
    without bound.

Add four helpers — validate_decode_bounds,
validate_symbol_ingress, validate_duplicate_symbol, and
validate_direct_buffer_capacity — and call them in order inside
receive_symbol:

  1. Up-front, reject transfer_length > max_object_size and k >
     config.source_symbols(max_object_size) with the existing
     DecodeError::{MemoryLimitExceeded, UnsupportedSourceBlockSize}.
  2. Ingress: reject zero-length source blocks receiving symbols at
     all, and verify symbol length matches symbol_size.
  3. Duplicate handling: if an existing symbol is present for this
     ESI, compare payloads byte-for-byte. Identical payloads return
     Ok(None) (benign duplicate). Conflicting payloads raise
     DecodeError::InvalidSymbol with reason including the ESI, so
     the caller can surface the adversarial case.
  4. Capacity: before insert, ensure we are still under the buffer
     cap.

encode.rs pulls in a minor signature tightening required by the new
bounds helpers on the encoder side.

No on-the-wire format changes; no existing legitimate decode path
should newly fail. The tests added elsewhere in this branch cover
the new rejection paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
